response.justification: Candidate fails to meet critical mandatory requirements for this capital markets C++ role. While they demonstrate strong software engineering capabilities, they lack the required deep C++ expertise (only ~6 months documented at National Instruments vs. 5-10 years mandatory), show no clear Windows platform development background, and lack demonstrated SQL Server/Oracle proficiency. Capital markets exposure is limited to 1.5 years at a generic FinTech firm with no fixed income or derivatives experience. This candidate is fundamentally a full-stack web/cloud engineer, not a C++ systems developer for capital markets.
strengths: Demonstrates exceptional software craftsmanship with 19 years of development across multiple languages and strong TDD/CI/CD practices
Some capital markets exposure at RIDE Capital with proven ability to learn complex financial systems
High-performance systems background (DLang async I/O, real-time data handling) shows potential for performance-critical trading systems
concerns: Critical C++ gap: only ~6 months documented experience (LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments) versus mandatory 5-10 years; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web technologies
No evidence of Windows platform development or SQL Server/Oracle database expertise; background predominantly Linux/cloud-based
Limited fixed income/derivatives domain knowledge; capital markets experience is generic FinTech without specialized financial products focus or investment banking exposure
